observations.summary: The e2e-tests step has an empty prompt and the agent halted 3 times to ask for direction when the story had no E2E work, despite prepare-tests having already deferred E2E in the ATDD checklist.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for e2e-tests (halts: 3, outcomeHistory: ["COMPLETE"], responseExcerpt: "work has been completed. nothing else is needed from you"). traces/4.11-a1-20260714T123043/e2e-tests.json narrative (13 of 58 parts) shows the agent asked "which direction would you like?" after determining Story 4.11 is a documentation+verification story with no E2E work — prepare-tests had already deferred E2E in the ATDD checklist ("No browser-level mock pattern can simulate the ACs"). The user had to respond 3 times before the step completed. The first halt was a genuine question; the subsequent 2 halts were the agent not cleanly completing after the user's response (saying only "Bye Marius!" and "👋"). traces/4.11-a1-20260714T123043/e2e-tests.json --include machinery --grep for HALT|QUESTION|reason returns 0 matches — the halt mechanism is not visible in the trace, but the narrative confirms a genuine question was posed. Playbook e2e-tests prompt is empty (""). Trends show e2e-tests has halts: 7 across 35 runs; haltsPerStory for 4.11 is 3.

observations.summary: create-story timed out (rc:124) with salvaged output; the 90-minute duration is 5x the step average, but the salvaged output was a coherent summary and the story file was created successfully.
observations.evidence: runner-errors.jsonl line 20: {"ts":"2026-07-14T14:00:44Z","source":"opencode-runner","context":"Create story (4.11) / initial run","rc":124,"salvagedOutput":"yes","stderrTail":""}. Journal step_end for create-story (durationMs: 5401899, ~90 min, 5x the 1002937ms average; status: success; halts: 0; outcomeHistory: ["COMPLETE"]; responseExcerpt is a coherent summary: "Deferred-work.md analysis complete... No deferred findings to pull in... Now let me create the comprehensive story file."). The timeout fired at 14:00:44 and step_end was at 14:00:46 (2 seconds later). Trends show infra-opencode-timeout has 11 distinct runs; ledger entries record prior occurrences on prepare-tests (4.2, 4.3, 4.9), review-code (3.9, 4.10), unit-tests (3.11), test-review (4.4), implement-story (4.5), NFR review (4.4), update-project-context (4.8), create-story (4.9).

observations.summary: review-code timed out (rc:124) with salvaged mid-stream output, but the substantive work was completed — review findings were written to the story file, 14 patches applied, and tests pass; the timeout only interrupted the final status update.
observations.evidence: runner-errors.jsonl line 21: {"ts":"2026-07-14T16:07:03Z","source":"opencode-runner","context":"Code review (4.11) / initial run","rc":124,"salvagedOutput":"yes","stderrTail":""}. Journal step_end for review-code (durationMs: 5402376, ~90 min; status: success; halts: 0; outcomeHistory: ["COMPLETE"]; responseExcerpt: "All 584 tests pass (581 original + 3 new tests added). Let me now update the story status and sync sprint tracking." — mid-stream, not a completion message). traces/4.11-a1-20260714T123043/review-code.json narrative (23 of 170 parts) shows the agent completed the review, triaged 14 patch / 6 defer / 4 dismiss findings, applied all 14 patches, and ran tests (all pass) before the timeout. Story file _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/4-11-configure-launch-window-monitoring-and-alerting.md:315-351 confirms the Review Findings section was written with all 14 patches, 6 deferred, 4 dismissed. Story file line 7 shows Status: done — the status was updated via tool calls after the last narrative message but before the timeout. This is a recurrence of infra-opencode-timeout (11 distinct runs per trends) but with minimal impact — unlike 4.10 where the timeout prevented any findings from being produced.

observations.summary: prepare-tests created a scaffold with ~15 quality issues (4 regex bugs, Bearer guard false positive, and ~10 more guard-design issues) that implement-story and review-code had to fix; prepare-tests did not consult sibling test files for established guard patterns.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for implement-story: "fixed 4 test regex bugs (missing `m` flag on multiline heading assertions), fixed Bearer guard false positive in runbook." traces/4.11-a1-20260714T123043/implement-story.json message msg_f61004ad00010aHGLfYjB4Vbth part 2 L3: "the regexes are missing the `m` flag (`^#+.*uptime.*monitor/i` without `m`), so `^` only matches the start of the string, not the start of each line. This is a test scaffold bug — I'll fix the regexes." and L4: "Bearer guard — the runbook says 'not as a Bearer header' which triggers `Bearer\s+(?![$"])` (Bearer followed by 'h'). I'll rephrase to avoid the false positive." traces/4.11-a1-20260714T123043/prepare-tests.json --grep for "custom-domain|sibling|bearer|Bearer|m flag|multiline|regex" returns 1 match (the final summary mentioning credential-isolation) — the agent never mentioned consulting sibling test files. Story file review-code section (lines 319-334) lists 14 patches, ~10 of which are test-scaffold quality issues: curl flags guard checks presence not per-command (line 322), curl flags guard passes vacuously (line 323), rollback independence guard checks globally not within section (line 324), UptimeRobot API key regex requires 10+ digits (line 325), Bearer guard doesn't exclude single quotes (line 326), 5-minute interval test checks prose not parameter (line 327), out-of-scope tests don't verify proximity (line 329), missing env var reference guards (line 330), missing placeholder guard (line 331), credential env-var guard regex lacks word boundary (line 334). Ledger entries 39 (3.8), 48 (3.10), 4.6, 4.10 record prior occurrences; trends show 11 distinct runs. The 4.10 finding identified the root cause as prepare-tests not consulting sibling test files for corrected guard patterns; no amendment was applied after 4.10.

observations.summary: 4.11 review-tests reported 0 stale markers across 114 test files; the 4.10 fix of 35 markers makes a clean result plausible, but the search-scope inconsistency confirmed in 4.10 persists as a risk — the agent's claim of comprehensive coverage is not verifiable from the output alone.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for review-tests: "All 114 test files across every test directory and every test file type... Stale transitional markers — 0 found." The responseExcerpt lists specific file types (Jest unit, integration, component .test.tsx, Playwright E2E, shared lib). Ledger entry 4.10 (2026-07-14T12:30:43.192Z) confirmed the search-scope inconsistency: review-tests found 35 markers across 24 files that 4.6 reported as 0 and 4.8 reported as 3 — all markers existed during those prior runs. The 4.10 fix of 35 markers likely cleaned the backlog, making 4.11's clean result plausible, but the 4.8 hypothesized observation (confirmed in 4.10) established that the agent's search execution varies in thoroughness despite correct prompt language.
observations.hypothesis: 4.11's search may have been genuinely comprehensive (4.10 fixed 35 markers, leaving none), or it may have missed markers that exist in files the agent did not actually examine — the claim of 114 files searched is not verifiable from the output alone, and the 4.10 finding confirmed this exact pattern of unverifiable comprehensive claims.
observations.nextStep: Widen into traces/4.11-a1-20260714T123043/review-tests.json with --include machinery to see what search method (grep/glob tool calls) the 4.11 agent used and how many files it actually examined; compare against the 114 files claimed. If the search method was comprehensive, this confirms the 4.10 fix cleaned the backlog; if not, the search-scope inconsistency persists and an update_step to review-tests requiring the agent to list all files searched before reporting results may be warranted.

observations.summary: The 4.3 update_step to implement-story is working — review-tests found 0 stale transitional markers for the second consecutive run (4.10 fixed 35, 4.11 found 0); implement-story correctly removed all transitional markers during the REFACTOR phase.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for review-tests: "Stale transitional markers — 0 found. None to fix — dev already cleaned up ATDD checklist + test file header." Journal step_end for implement-story: "Removed all `test.skip()` markers" (no mention of leaving transitional markers). Journal step_end for prepare-tests: "Implementation Note 4 updated from 'No ATDD test scaffold exists' to 'ATDD test scaffold already applied'" and "ATDD Artifacts section added to Dev Notes with checklist path and activation instructions." Playbook implement-story prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-12T15:50:56.335Z) includes "remove all transitional phase markers from test-file headers and test planning artifacts (such as ATDD checklists) — any comment, header, or status line that describes a TDD phase transition (whether claiming tests are skipped, disabled, in a red-phase, or in a green-phase state) must be removed entirely rather than relabeled." Ledger entries 146 (4.6, deduced non-recurrence), 154 (4.8, deduced non-recurrence), 4.10 (deduced non-recurrence) confirm the update_step is working for current-story files.

observations.summary: The 3.9 update_step is working — implement-story proactively applied NFR patterns; the 2 NFR findings (MEDIUM and LOW) are content-specific gaps in a documentation story (missing test guard for --fail limitation note, missing rate limit documentation), not gaps in applying documented project-context.md patterns.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for review-nfrs: "2 findings — NFR-1 (MEDIUM, reliability): No test guard for the `--fail` limitation note. NFR-2 (LOW, reliability): Runbook doesn't document UptimeRobot rate limit." Both fixes applied directly by review-nfrs. Journal step_end for implement-story: "All curl commands include --fail and --max-time 30 flags (project-context.md rule)" — implement-story applied the documented curl-flags pattern. The MEDIUM finding is about runbook content (documentation of a limitation), not a code pattern from project-context.md. Playbook implement-story prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-12T15:50:56.335Z) includes "Before marking the story complete, re-read project-context.md and verify each non-functional pattern documented there has been applied to your changes where applicable." Ledger entries 5, 37, 43, 52, 59, 4.1, 4.2, 4.8, 4.10 record prior occurrences and non-recurrence.

Tasks 2.1 and 2.2 marked [x] (scaffold already applied by ATDD)... Task 2.3 amended to instruct removing test.skip() after creating the runbook... Implementation Note 4 updated from 'No ATDD test scaffold exists' to 'ATDD test scaffold already applied'... ATDD Artifacts section added to Dev Notes with checklist path and activation instructions." Ledger entries 38, 55, 130, 136, 158, 4.10 record prior occurrences and non-recurrence.

observations.summary: The 3.7 update_step is working — prepare-tests performed the browser-level mock feasibility check and correctly deferred all ACs (infrastructure verification requirements: uptime monitoring, log access, deploy notification).
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for prepare-tests: "E2E deferral check: No browser-level mock can simulate infrastructure verification ACs (uptime monitoring configured, logs accessible, deploy notification reaches operator). Deferred — recorded in ATDD checklist." Journal step_end for e2e-tests: the agent confirmed "No browser-level mock pattern can simulate the ACs for Story 4.11. The acceptance criteria are infrastructure verification requirements... not user-facing UI behavior." Ledger entry 33 (3.7) records original observation; 3.11, 3.12, 4.1, 4.2, 4.8, 4.10 deduce non-recurrence.

observations.summary: The 4.6 update_step is working — both NFR findings were introduced by the current story and fixed directly; no MEDIUM+ findings needed deferral to deferred-work.md.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for review-nfrs: "I applied both fixes directly." NFR-1 (MEDIUM) and NFR-2 (LOW) were both introduced by the current story with straightforward remediation. The prompt directs deferral only for MEDIUM+ findings that are not fixed in this step; since both were fixed, no deferral was needed. Playbook review-nfrs prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-13T11:15:48.883Z) includes "For any MEDIUM or higher finding that is not fixed in this step, record it in @_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md." Ledger entry 4.6 records the applied update_step; ledger entries 4.8, 4.10 deduce non-recurrence.

observations.summary: The 4.6 update_step is working — the 13 project-wide skips are all conditional with still-applicable environmental dependencies; no always-skipped tests persist.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for review-tests: "Skipped tests (project-wide) — 13 found, all conditional. None removed — all skip reasons still applicable." The 13 remaining skips are conditional (CI tier separation, external service credentials, external test resources) — none fall into the 'no longer applicable' categories. Playbook review-tests prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-14T00:00:00.000Z) includes "For always-skipped tests with no condition that cannot run in any CI tier, remove them directly or convert to conditional skips — do not just flag them." Ledger entry 4.6 records the applied update_step; ledger entries 4.8, 4.10 deduce non-recurrence.

amendments.prompt: Before generating E2E tests, check the story's ATDD checklist for any E2E deferral decision. If E2E coverage was deferred by prepare-tests because no browser-level mock can simulate the acceptance criteria, confirm the deferral is still valid and complete the step without generating tests or asking for direction. For any decision that arises, consult @_bmad-output/decision-policy.md before escalating: if a rule covers it, decide and continue. HALT only for decisions no rule covers.
amendments.rationale: The e2e-tests step has an empty prompt, and the agent halted 3 times to ask for direction when E2E was already deferred by prepare-tests — directing the agent to check the ATDD checklist and handle deferrals autonomously eliminates the unnecessary halts.

amendments.prompt: Create tests. After applying TDD red-phase scaffolding (adding skipped test blocks to new or existing files, adding test seams, or creating stub files), update the story file's tasks to reflect what was already done — tasks that instruct the dev to create scaffolding that prepare-tests has already applied should be amended to instruct activation of the existing scaffolding instead, so the story does not contradict the codebase state. Before deferring E2E coverage, verify no browser-level mock pattern can simulate the scenario — only defer if no mock covers the ACs, and record the check in the ATDD checklist. When creating regression guards for code that executes external commands with user-controlled input, apply a uniform guard template to every call site: exercise both credential-isolation invariants (no credentials leak via command arguments or environment variables) and input-injection invariants (malicious input is safely quoted and cannot alter the command's behavior). Before creating regression guard tests, consult sibling test files in the same directory for established guard patterns and regex conventions — patterns corrected during prior code reviews may exist in sibling files and should be applied rather than reintroduced. For any decision that arises, consult @_bmad-output/decision-policy.md before escalating: if a rule covers it, decide and continue. HALT only for decisions no rule covers.
amendments.rationale: prepare-tests created a scaffold with ~15 quality issues that implement-story and review-code had to fix; consulting sibling test files for established guard patterns would avoid reintroducing bugs already fixed in prior reviews.
